sutta: monks these 9 persons are venerable worthy of honor worthy of gifts worthy of salutations the world's unsurpassed field for making merit the arahant he who has entered on the path to arahantship the anagamin or non-returner he was entered on the path to the realization of the fruit of the non-returner the sakata gaming of 1's returner he was entered on the path to the realization of the fruit of the once the sotapanna of stream winner he was entered on the path to the realization of the fruit of the stream winner and he who has become 1 of the clan godrabu monks these 9 persons are vulnerable worthy of honor worthy of gifts worthy of salutations the world's unsurpassed feel for making merit that's the end of the sutta
commentary: what is interesting about this uttar is that it mentions the 8 aryans plus a person called the gotra gotra now according to the command commentary the boo is 1 endowed with exceedingly powerful insight and thought with immediate prospects of attaining to the state of the stream winner and the way which means that the gotra boom of 1 of the clan is not an aryan he's not an aryan but he's very close to becoming a stream winner he he is fam fairly familiar with the sutas so even that type of person is considered vulnerable worthy of honor worthy of gifts worthy of salutations the world's unsurpassed feel for merit for making merit so these are the 9 persons who are described as such the next bhutan is

sutta: once when the exalted 1 was staying near sabahti at jeter grove in anata pindika's park the venerable sariputta before known dressed and with bowl and rope went towards sabahti then thought he too early is it too early is it yet to go about for arms in sabahti suppose i were to go and visit the park of the wondrous of other views so the wearable sarikuta went to their power and after exchanging with them the usual compliments sat down at 1 side now at that time this happened to be the topic of their talk as they gathered around all who die with some attach remainder are not all together freed from health rebirth in the womb of an animal the realm of ghosts nor from the untoward way the ill way the abyss hell and the verbal sariputta neither applauded nor dispersed their speech but without command rose from his seat and departed saying to himself i will learn the truth of the matter from the exalted 1 so the verbal sariputta after going about for arms in in savati when he had returned there from and eaten his meal visited the exalted 1 and after saluting sat down at 1 side so seated he told the exalted 1 all that had taken place the buddha said sariputta some of these wanderers of other views are fools and without understanding but some will know of 1 who has attachment he has some attached remainder and some will know of 1 who has no attachment he has none sarikuta there are these 9 persons who when they die with some attached remainder are all together freed from hell rebirth in the womb of an animal the realm of course what 9 consider sariputta the person who is accomplished in the precepts accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom he destroys the 5 lower factors and becomes completely cool after an interval this sarikuta is the first person who dying with some attached remainder is all together free from hell rebirth in the womb of an animal the realm of course again sariputta consider the person who is accomplished in the precepts accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom he destroys the 5 lower factors and com becomes completely cool after lessening his period again consider the person who is accomplishing the precepts accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom it destroys the 5 lower factors and becomes completely cool without exertion again sorry putter consider the person who is accomplished in the precepts accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom he destroys the 5 lower factors and becomes completely cool after some exertion against considered the person who is accomplishing the precepts accomplished in wisdom is already accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom he destroys the 5 lower factors and he ascends the stream of existence up to the akanita heaven this sariputta is the fifth person who dying with some attached remainder is all together freed from hell the rebirth in the womb of animal the realm of course i stop here for a moment to commend here the buddha is talking about 9 persons who died [Music] with some attach remained that means they have not attained um and here the first 5 the buddha is talking about well is there are 5 types and we came across these 5 types earlier in this angutta nikaya 7.6.52 and and so i think we don't need to discuss them here and this anagamin is 1 here the buddha says is accomplishing the precepts accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom in other words he has perfect moral conduct perfect concentration that means 1 pointness of mind but not perfect wisdom and the sutta continues again sariputta consider the person who is accomplished in the precepts but not in concentration nor in wisdom by destroying the 3 factors and reducing lust hatred and delusion he becomes 1's returner sakadagamin who returns to this world once again only once again and makes an end of ill this sarphuta is the sixth person again saraputta considered the person who is accomplished in the precepts but not in concentration nor in wisdom by destroying the tree fatters he becomes 1 germinating only once more a carbegin and on being born into the state of man just once he makes an end of ill this sariputta is the seventh person against consider the person who is accomplished in the precepts is moderately successful in concentration is moderately successful in wisdom by destroying the 3 factors he becomes a clan to clan goal colon cola and running on very on through 2 or 3 clans he makes an end of ill this sariputta is the eighth person again sariputta considered the person who is accomplishing the precepts and is moderately successful in concentration moderately successful in wisdom by destroying the free 3 factors he becomes 1 who will be reborn 7 times at most and running on fearing on among devas and men for 7 times at most he makes an end of idle this sarphuta is the ninth person who dying with some attached remainder is altogether free from hell a rebirth in the boom of an animal from the realm of course some wonders of other views are fools and without understanding but some will know of 1 who has attachment he has some attached remainder and some will know of 1 who has no attachment he has none verily sariputta these 9 persons who die with some remained are freed from hell rebirth in the womb of an animal the realm of course not until now sarikuta has this dhamma discourse been declared to monk or none to lay men or lay women and why lest after hearing this dharma discourse they bring on themselves the habit of eyebrowness sarikuta this dhamma discourse was only declared by me because i'm of my being questioned that's the end of the suta
commentary:
so the last 4 types of persons discuss here the first 1 is the sakata garment 1's returner who is who has perfect moral conduct but not perfect concentration and wisdom he destroys the 3 factors and has reduced lust hatred and delusion and he returns to this world that means as a human being only once more and then the other 3 asutapanas stream and teresa they have all destroyed the 3 factors they are also perfect in moral conduct but not in concentration and wisdom and so here you can see that sulta panna and this akata gum in that they don't need perfect concentration that means they don't need jhana they don't need 1 pointedness of mine [Music] now the sotapanna is reborn a maximum of 7 times only among devas and men and the other thing mentioned here is that the buddha said some wonders of other views are fools and without understanding but some will know of 1 who has attachment and some will know of 1 who has no attachment which means that not all external ascetics are ignorant some also know and at the last part the buddha said not until now sarikuta has this dharma discourse been declared to monk or none to lay men or lay person and why lest after hearing this dharma this course they bring up themselves the habit of idealness so the buddha here says that at first he did not want to talk about [Music] this um detail because here he's saying uh for example that uh sutapana and asaka tagamin first and seconds fruition persons don't need jhana and in the majjimanikaya 43 the buddha said that the right view can be attained only by 2 conditions with only 2 conditions 1 is listening to the dharma and the other 1 is happy having yoniso manasikhara proper attention so you can see that sotapanna can be attained by listening to the dhamma only and the buddha did not want to say this because the buddha doesn't want his aryan disciples who are not yet to be idle he wants them to finish their work in this very life that's why the buddha did not give the details of this utah earlier

items: The person who is accomplished in the precepts, accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom
The person who is accomplished in the precepts, accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom (after lessening his period)
The person who is accomplishing the precepts, accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom (without exertion)
The person who is accomplished in the precepts, accomplished in concentration but not in wisdom (after some exertion)
The person who is accomplished in the precepts, accomplished in wisdom
The person who is accomplished in the precepts, but not in concentration nor in wisdom (returner)
The person who is accomplished in the precepts, but not in concentration nor in wisdom (germinating only once more)
The person who is moderately successful in the precepts, moderately successful in concentration, and moderately successful in wisdom
The person who is accomplishing the precepts, and is moderately successful in concentration, and moderately successful in wisdom

sutta: the buddha said i will teach you these 9 attainments of gradual abiding listen pay heed i will speak and what monks are the 9 attainments where sense desires end and those who have ended since desires so abide surely those bearable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do sense desires end and who having ended since desires so abide i know not this i see not this who should speak thus to him this ought to be said consider reverend sir the monk who allude from sen's desires aloof from unwholesome states enters and abides in the first jhana which is accompanied by applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of seclusion their sense desires end and those who have ended sense desires so abide surely the guideless man without deceit would rejoice and be glad at this response and say well said and thereafter with join hands honor and reverence him just stop here for a moment so here the buddha buddha is talking actually about jhana states and states of concentration and in these states of concentration starting from the first jhana sense desires end and the buddha says that where sense desires and and those who have ended since desires so abide surely those valuable men craving not and cool nibuta have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond paragata this statement they have become cool nebuta and have crossed over and gone beyond paragata is normally used for the arahant [Music] supposed to have become enlightened and the buddha is using this for the jhana states which shows the great um respect and the great uh generation that the buddha puts in the jhana states states of concentration and then later the buddha goes on to discriminate the first jhana the state of the first ghana is um among allu from sen's desires allu from unwholesome states enters and abides in the first jhana so to be able to enter the first jhana you have to be aloof or secluded from sense desires aloof or secluded from unwholesome states which means that the first jhana is a statement which is totally wholesome holy wholesome because it is uh aloof from unwholesome states so here the buddha says that um if you after hearing uh this that the person who abides in the first jhana their sense desires and and the 1 who has ended since desires abides in that state then that person then anyone who hears that i should rejoice and be glad and should honor and reverence him and the buddha continues where thought applied and sustained and those who have ended such thoughts so abide surely those variable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the man who with the stilling of applied and sustained thought enters and abides in the second jhana which has internal self-confidence and singleness of mind without applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of concentration surely the godless man would rejoice at this response so stop here so in the state of the second jana the buddha says the person has to to still apply it and sustain thought applied and sustain thoughts and have to end before he can enter the second jana which has internal self-confidence and singleness of mind without applied and sustained thought but with delight and pleasure born of concentration [Music] then the buddha continues where delight ends and those who have ended delight so abide surely those vulnerable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the monk who with the fading away of the life abides in equanimity and mindful and aware enters and abides in the third ghana and experiences pleasure with his body whereof the aryans declare he that is equanimous and mindful has a joyful abiding there the light ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the godless man would rejoice at this response and stop here again so here the state of the third jhana you see that delight has to fade away and among um when he enters in abides in the third jana he is said to be equanimous and mindful and has a joyful abiding so you see the state of the third jana is very clear that it is a mindful statement because some people they have a mistaken idea that jhanas are states of unmindfulness as though in a trance but states of jhana are in 10 states of mindfulness the mind is very clear and very strong and equanimous and because the mind is equanimous and that is why there is no tendency to lust for jhana unlike sensual pleasures sensual pleasures and there is delight but the mind is not equanimous and because of that the mind is very active there is a tendency to lust for that kind of pleasure but not with states of high concentration [Music] and then the buddha continues when equanimous pleasure ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those vulnerable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide considers the mong who with the abandoning of bodily pleasure and the previous abandoning of bodily pain by the fading away of joy and grief he enters and abides in the fourth jana which has complete purity of mindfulness and equanimity and has neither pain nor pleasure then equanimous pleasure ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the guideless man would rejoice at this response stop here again for a moment so in the state of the fourth jhana bodily pleasure and bodily pain and mental joy and mental grief have ended before that person can enter into the fourth jhana which is a state of complete purity of mindfulness and equanimity so here again now you see here that the fourth jana is the state of perfect mindfulness it is not an unmindful statement and the buddha continues where perceptions of form end and those who abide surely those variable men craving not and cool have but at very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the mong who unattentive to the perceptions of the many fall enters and abides in the sphere of infinite space their perceptions of form and and those who have ended them so abide surely the guideless man would rejoice at this response and stop here again for a moment so here the buddha is talking about the the first arupa jana which is the sphere of infinite space here the mind opens up to infinite space so that the mind becomes boundless in a way and in this state now perceptions of the many fold meaningful the bollywood is nanata and it also means diversity here the manga is unattentive he's not aware of perceptions of diversity that means he doesn't see all beings as individuals it doesn't consider all individual beings or as individuals and this is the beginning of the perception of no self the mind opens up and it refuses though all beings are 1 in his mind this is why china states are very important for insight for insider the higher wisdom because when a person comes out of those states the the insights are not the same as a person who uses the normal mind then the buddha continues where the perception of the sphere of infinite space ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those vulnerable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the monk who thinking consciousness is infinite enters and abides in the sphere of infinite consciousness there the perception of the sphere of infinite space ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the godless man would rejoice at this response where the perception of the sphere of infinite consciousness ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those vulnerable men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the man who thinking there is nothing enters and abides in the sphere of nothingness there the perception of the sphere of infinite conscious consciousness ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the guideless man would rejoice at this response where the perception of the sphere of nothingness ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those verbal men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where do they end and who having ended them so abide consider sir the monk who passing wholly beyond the sphere of nothingness enters and abides in the sphere of neither perception or non-perception there the perception of the sphere of nothingness ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the guideless man would rejoice at this response where the perception of the sphere of neither perception nor non-perception ends and those who have ended it so abide surely those verbal men craving not and cool have by that very fact crossed over and gone beyond i say where ends the perception of this fear and who having ended it so abide i know not this i see not this who should speak thus to him this ought to be said consider reverend sir the man who passing wholly beyond that sphere enters and abides in the ending of perception and feeling there the perception of the sphere of neither perception nor non-perception ends and those who have ended it so abide surely the guideless man would without deceit would rejoice and be glad that this response and say well said and thereafter with join hands honor and reverence him verily monks these are the 9 attainments of gradual abiding that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so after that the buddha goes on to the arupa jana called the sphere of infinite consciousness in that state the mind the consciousness becomes infinite and a person who has this kind of statement he can know things that happen in far away places because the mind opens up and he has infinite consciousness and then this fear of nothingness in that state he realizes that everything is like mind made that there is nothing is all the whole universe is mind made exists in consciousness and basically there is nothing it is like imagination and then after that this fear of neither perception or non-perception where the mind is so uh still that it's almost the consciousness is almost about to be like a small flame about to to be blown off so sometimes that person is like and another time means as to why he doesn't perceive this why it's called the sphere of neither perception nor non-perception and the final state now is the state where perception and feeling ends when perception and feeling ends then the normal sex consciousness also ends and so these are the 9 states where the buddha praises now the next few sutas all have to do with states of ghana concentration the next 1 is

sutta: [Music] 1 is the verbal sariputta dwelt near rajya gaha at the squirrel's feeding ground in the bamboo grove and there he addressed the monk saying this school nibana reverences his happiness this cool references his happiness now when he had spoken thus the vulnerable udain said to him but what hearing reverence is the happiness which hearing is not sensed stop here for a moment nibana is the state now where the senses have stopped them so this mangudais wants the variable sarikuta to and the variables should sarkuta continue it is very very just that happiness which hearing is not sensed there are references these 5 strands of sense desire what 5 there are forms cognized by the eye longed for alluring pleasurable lovely bound up with passion and desire there are sounds cognized by the ear smells by the nose tastes by the tongue contacts or tangibles recognized by the touch longed for alluring pleasurable lovely bound up with passion and desire these ribbons are the 5 strands of sense desire and the happiness the well-being arising there from is called sensual happiness consider reverence the monk who allude from sense desire aloo from unwholesome states enters and abides in the first jhana if while he abides in that abiding perceptions and thoughts accompanied by sense desire beset him it is indeed for him a disease just as some ill amounting to a disease might arise in a happy person even so those perceptions and thoughts accompanied by sense desire beset him and indeed it is for him a disease now disease is called ill by the exalted 1 verily reverend sir it is just in this way that the cool nirvana ought to be understood as happiness stop here for a moment so here uh the variable salary putter is explaining that when among enters the first ghana he abides in a very peaceful and a very blissful abiding and if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by sense desire should be set him it sort of gets him out of the first jana and it is like a happy person becoming sick so that is why um some disease so so uh saying that the cool banner ought to be understood as happiness in this way in other words he is likening the state of the first jhana itself to bana is very happy blissful abiding and he continues again consider the monk who enters and abides in the second jhana his perceptions and thoughts accompanied by application that means applied beside him disease is just this way that the could talk to in the third journal exceptions and the thoughts accompanied by delight pt beside him or in the fourth jhana if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by pain beset him or in this or in the sphere of infinite space if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by form beset him or in the sphere of infinite consciousness if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by the sphere of infinite space beset him or in the sphere of nothingness if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by the sphere of infinite consciousness beset him or in the sphere of neither perception or non-perception if perceptions and thoughts accompanied by this fear of nothingness beset him it is indeed for him a disease filled ill amounting to a disease might arise in a happy person even so those things beset him and indeed they are for him diseases now disease is called ill by the exalted 1 verily reverence sir it is just in this way that the cool ought to be understood as happiness and then reverence consider the monk who passing wholly beyond the sphere of neither perception or non-perception enters and abides in the ending of perception and feeling when by wisdom he sees that the asavas are completely destroyed clearly reference it is just in this way that the cool ought to be understood as happiness that's the end of the sutta
commentary:
so similarly variable sarikuta is saying if a person abides in the jhana and some perceptions and thoughts accompanied by a lower state arises so that it moves him out him out of that ghana state it is just like a happy man becoming sigma he's he's moved out of that blissful state now so those things that move him out of the blissful state are a disease so that is why the verbal sarikuta is giving the similaria that when the manga bites in the jhanas it is it is such a blissful happy statement similar to nirvana in a way yeah so you can see the arahants and the buddha themselves they sometimes liken the jhana states to nibana

sutta: 4 uh liberated by wisdom liberated by wisdom he is called and as to what has the 1 liberated by wisdom been declared by the exalted 1 and the allu from sen's desire enters and abides in the first ghana and by wisdom understands it thus faster in 1 particular has the 1 liberated by wisdom been declared by the exalted 1 similarly for the second janata jana for jhana she of knight of the sphere of infinite space fear of infinite consciousness nothingness neither perception nor non-perception ending of perception and feeling now so
commentary: this suta is talking about the motto is the 1 liberated by wisdom and this refers to an arahant and just now i mentioned aryans mentioned in the kitagiri 2 of them are harahans 1 1 is this 1 liberated by wisdom and liberated by wisdom means a person attains the janus as mentioned in this he attains the he has he has attained the janus but when he is liberated at the moment of liberation he does not use the janus for example [Music] and 1 day when the buddha was talking to an external ascetic parabol sariputta stood by the side of the buddha and took a fan and was fanning the buddha and he was fanning the buddha he was listening to the conversation between the buddha and the external ascetic and when he was suddenly he attained insider and he attained liberation so such a person is called liberated by wisdom because at the moment of liberation the faculty of wisdom is strong the strongest he has he's not using jhana at that moment he's using wisdom that's why he said to be liberated by wisdom but even here as is mentioned in this utah even 1 who is liberated by wisdom still needs the first jana or the second or the third or the fourth jana or the other janas the arupa janas so jhanas are always needed for liberation but it is interesting here that even a person who attains the first jhana can attain liberation but this is not always the case usually the buddha mentions that the fourth jana is needed but sometimes in certain persons whose faculty of wisdom is very strong even with the first jana they can become an arahant the next is

sutta: once when the melba ananda was dwelling near kosambi in gosita the very visited him and after exchanging the usual polite talk sat down at 1 side and so seated he said to the verbal ananda this robin sir was said by the deva panchala chanda from news escape he found quick sage who walked to jhana awaken seer a depth withdrawn now what sir is the news and what the the way of escape declared by the exalted and november ananda replied these 5 strands of sense desire revenge are called the nose by the exalted 1 forms cognized by the eye long for alluring pleasurable lovely bound up with passion and desire sounds recognized by the institutes by the tongue contacts by the touch long for alluring pleasurable lovely bound up with passion and desire these 5 strands of sense desire are called the nose by the exalted 1 consider a reverend sir the monk who aloud from sen's desire enters and abides in the first jhana thus far reverend sir in 1 particular did the exalted 1 declare a way of escape from the nose and there too is the nose and what is there insofar as thought applied and sustained is not ended that there is the nose now consider sir the monk who enters and abides in the second jhana the third the fourth in the sphere of infinite space of infinite consciousness of nothingness of neither perception or non-perception the ending of perception and feeling and ceased by wisdom that the asavas are completely destroyed thus far and with no further particular did the exalted 1 did clear a way of escape from the nose that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here the monk is asking the rebel ananda what is the nose the nose is like the rope that is tied around 1's neck when you're going to hang somebody here you put a nose around his neck this rope around his neck so [Music] the 5 strands of sense desire forms sounds smells taste and touch these are the these are the things that bind us like a nose on around our neck we are caught by mara mara has put a rope around our neck and we are in control of mara because of the 5 senses and the way of escapement that the buddha has declared now is the first jhana the second jana third fought jhana sphere of infinite space of infinite consciousness nothingness neither perception or non-perception and in the ending of perception and feeling these are the ways of escapement from mara's nose and and this is another suta that shows how important the janas are if you want to escape from the nose that mara has put around our neck so definitely the janas are the teachings of the buddhas and sutas the buddha has declared that the way of escape or from liberation from samsara and from mara is by attaining the janas because from the jaanas when we attain the janus the 5 hindrances are abandoned and when the 5 indians are abandoned then we see things clearly as they really are and from there we get inside wisdom then next is
